item.title: New mRNA flu vaccine far more effective than existing jabs, but has some limitations, study finds
item.description: A newly developed mRNA-based flu vaccine is almost 35 per cent more effective against the most common form of influenza, according to a new study.

item.title: ‘The zombies have evolved’: Interview with Resident Evil Requiem producer
item.description: Capcom, the developers of the classic Resident Evil franchise, are gearing up for their ninth major entry in the long running series — Resident Evil Requiem. 9news.com.au caught up with the game’s producer.

item.title: 'Double-edged sword': How Aussie content creators are reacting to the under-16 social media ban
item.description: A﻿s the under-16 social media ban inches closer, questions are being raised about the implications the ban will have on people who target their content toward younger audiences.

item.title: Everything we know about ChatGPT's 'erotica' rollout in December
item.description: Experts from a range of fields, including OpenAI's own wellbeing panel, are worried about what it means for mental health.

item.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
item.description: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

item.title: How Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora's From the Ashes expansion aligns with the new film
item.description: Developed in collaboration between Massive Entertainment and Lightstorm Entertainment, the From the Ashes game expansion runs side by side to the events of Avatar: Fire and Ash.

item.title: Apple issues urgent iPhone update after triple zero emergency call testing raises concerns
item.description: Apple has released an emergency update to iOS 26 that applies only to the iPhone 12, addressing a compatibility issue with Australia's emergency calling system.

item.title: The Oscars to abandon broadcast TV, leaving longtime home of US network ABC
item.description: In a seismic shift for one of television’s marquee events, the Academy Awards will depart broadcast TV.

item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: The biggest video games set to be released this year
item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.title: We got an early look at three of the biggest games coming to Switch 2. This is our verdict
item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: Australia Post wants to raise stamp prices
item.description: T﻿he national postal service says it's needed to offset losses in its letter division.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: NSW to get an extra public holiday for the next two years after Anzac Day, Premier confirms
item.description: T﻿he decision hasn't been universally popular, but Minns insists the decision is the right thing to do.

item.title: Higher GST, lower income tax, part of plan to lift Australia's economy
item.description: Strong economic medicine is needed over d﻿ebt levels racked up by Australian states, a global body warns.

item.title: Australian food court chain Sushi Sushi sells to Japanese dining empire in estimated $160 million deal
item.description: Sushi Sushi, which began in Melbourne in 1998, has been snapped ﻿up by Tokyo-based Genki Global Dining Concepts.

item.title: Australian gas producer Santos wins court fight over net zero claims
item.description: A lawsuit against gas producer Santos alleged the company misled the public on its plans to achieve net zero carbon emissions.

item.title: Record number of cafes and restaurants going out of business
item.description: T﻿he hospitality industry in Australia is now split between winners and losers, new research shows.

item.title: 'This is peak Australia': NAB chief warns living standards will stagnate without serious reform
item.description: After generations of improvement, our living standards will be stuck in the present – or go backwards – without major economic reform.

item.title: Business busted for making false claims about link to Bondi terror attack
item.description: N﻿SW Fair Trading found the company falsely claimed the proceeds of sales would go to victims and their families.

item.title: Telstra records billion dollar profit, reveals it shed 2356 jobs in 2025
item.description: T﻿he job cuts have saved $118 million alone.

item.title: eBay to buy Depop in $1.7b move to reach Gen Z and Millennials
item.description: e﻿Bay hopes buying Depop will help it reach a younger and more fashion-forward audience.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Australians aren't eating enough burritos. It just cost one company hundreds of millions of dollars
item.description: I﻿ts share price has dropped by more than half in the last 12 months.

item.title: Employees at Aussie start-up Eucalyptus set to receive windfall after $1.6 billion acquisition
item.description: Employee shareholders at an Aussie start-up are set for a big pay day after the company was acquired for $1.6 billion.

item.title: WiseTech Global set to cut up to 2000 jobs for 'AI efficiency'
item.description: T﻿he software company said ongoing developments in AI would deliver a leaner organisation.

item.title: Domino's Pizza sheds 10 per cent of customers after reducing discounts
item.description: Domino's started offering fewer discounts, and some of its customers weren't too happy.

item.title: Man fired after pouring cooking oil down the drain outside popular bakery
item.description: The incident quickly gained attention, with members of the community saying the man's actions were "pure laziness", "disgusting", and ﻿"such a shame".

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Glue Store to close in shake-up by retail giant
item.description: The company said it now wants to focus on its global brands.

item.title: Qantas unveils major change to frequent flyer program as airline records bumper $1.46 billion profit
item.description: Qantas has announced major changes to its frequent flyer program after posting a bumper $1.46 billion half-year profit.

item.title: Company behind Australian cultural icon Hills Hoist could soon be sold
item.description: The company behind one of Australia's most iconic brands is set to be put up for sale as its American owner focuses on operations closer to home.

item.title: Almost 40,000 Budget Direct customers allegedly overcharged $3.3 million
item.description: Australia's financial watchdog has taken legal action against the insurer behind the Budget Direct products.

item.title: VB pins hopes on mid-strength as Aussies drop classic beers
item.description: T﻿he newly-unveiled mid-strength brew signals a growing trend across Australia as illustrated by the sales numbers.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: Australia's gender pay gap is creeping down, but some major Australian employers are lagging
item.description: Many major Australian brands are failing to turn the dial on the gender pay gap, with some even slipping backwards in the past year, a detailed new report has revealed.

item.title: Aussie pubs, jobs safe for now as UK company lays off hundreds of staff
item.description: The company has four breweries and about 100 pubs around the globe, including five here in Australia.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Australia's most trusted and distrusted brands revealed as local favourites dominate
item.description: S﻿ome brands soared and others struggled.

item.title: Business owner sends premier $24,000 bill after new public holiday announced
item.description: "I'm not arguing against a public holiday, but we were given four months' notice, so we never budgeted for this in our costings."

item.title: Australian markets suffer $90 billion wipeout as experts issue stark warning
item.description: At one stage today, the ASX had sunk by as much as 4.3 per cent, which would have put it on track for the steepest single-day drop since the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.title: Lululemon fined more than $700,000 over marketing emails
item.description: A﻿CMA found more than 370,000 emails the company sent were in breach of Australian law.

item.title: Aussie software giant Atlassian cuts 1600 jobs in push to AI
item.description: The CEO, one of Australia's richest men, said employees would get an email "within 20 minutes" to find out if they had lost their jobs.

item.title: Australia's No.1 retailer named as last year's winner plunges
item.description: T﻿he new king of brick-and-mortar retailers has been decided.

item.title: Man in intensive care after brawl at Melbourne pizza shop
item.description: A Melbourne man is in intensive care after a brawl broke out at a pizza shop in front of shocked diners.

item.description: T﻿ech company bosses have had similar things to say about AI.

item.title: One Nation surges in the polls, stripping potential votes from Labor and the Coalition
item.description: The federal opposition fell to a record low of 22 per cent.

item.title: One Nation's popularity is surging. But what does Pauline Hanson's party actually stand for?
item.description: As the firebrand Queenslander strips voters from Coalition and Labor, we take a deep dive into her politics.

item.title: Atlassian accused of illegally firing employee who suggested boss was 'rich jerk'
item.description: P﻿rosecutors argued that the employee was merely acting in the company's "Open Company, No Bullshit" philosophy.

item.title: Government doesn't rule out fuel rationing possibility, but says supplies still arriving as normal
item.description: It comes as major fuel companies have been hauled in front of the consumer watchdog for a please-explain.

item.description: "There are hundreds of thousands of people who don't use their firearms for the reason on their licence."

item.title: Iconic Stonewall Hotel goes under after 28 years as administrators appointed
item.description: The owner said it was a "sad and difficult" decision to appoint administrators. ﻿

item.title: Government drops clearest hints yet controversial tax break to be wound back
item.description: The federal government has dropped its two clearest hints to date it is preparing to wind back the capital gains tax (CGT) discount for property investors in the upcoming budget.

item.description: "The Iranian regime are engaging in frankly random attacks across the Gulf region."

item.description: T﻿he shock jock said ARN "decided to try and burn the place down" over an on-air spat with Jackie O.

item.description: The prime minister will talk to state premiers and chief ministers today in a national cabinet meeting aimed at ensuring Australia has a united response to the global oil supply shock.

item.description: T﻿he concerning data comes as Jacinta Allan insists speculation about a leadership coup is "anonymous speculation".

item.title: Rail union calls for free public transport as fuel crisis continues
item.description: Australians are paying about $30 more for a tank of unleaded petrol, and $70 for a tank of diesel.

item.title: One Nation to join South Australian lower house after surging support in election
item.description: One Nation has decimated the Liberals and claimed its first lower house seat won at an election outside of Queensland.

item.description: It comes after Trump threatened to send in immigration officers amid a funding shutdown.

item.title: How many days does Australia have left of fuel?
item.description: The government has insisted there is no issue of fuel supply, but how much fuel is actually left in Australia?

item.title: Motorists urged to use cheaper alternative at the bowser amid fuel concerns
item.description: Motorists are being encouraged to use a cheaper alternative at the bowser amid concern about fuel prices﻿ skyrocketing.

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies aged 43
item.description: Radvinsky transformed the British-based company into an online pornography powerhouse.

item.title: Cheaper cars, forbidden names, and more trade for farmers: Australia finalises long-awaited $10 billion-a-year
item.description: E﻿ight years after negotiations started, Canberra has signed a free-trade deal with the European Union.

item.title: Man charged for allegedly smashing NSW Parliament House window with barbell
item.description: A man has been arrested after a NSW Parliament House window was smashed with a metal barbell early ﻿this morning.

item.title: Why we can continue to call our cheese feta, but fontina will be a no-go
item.description: T﻿he names used in dozens of common goods will change under new rules in the recently inked Australia-European Union trade agreement.

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: Experts liken sky-high fuel prices to three interest rate rises
item.description: Experts are warning the economic shock from sky-high fuel prices is the equivalent of three interest rate rises.

item.title: Fines for dodgy petrol pricing doubled as fuel crisis continues
item.description: T﻿he government is trying to respond to growing anxiety about the fuel crisis sparked by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands addresses media after exiting court in $100 million showdown with ARN
item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.title: 'Businesses are going under now': Transport body knocks Albo's fuel crisis plan
item.description: T﻿he peak body for road transport says fresh national measures to combat the burgeoning fuel crisis fail to address the immediate needs for struggling operators.

item.title: Martina turned a childhood pastime into a seven-figure career
item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.description: The figures don't paint an overly pretty picture for Australia – although the government says there's a good reason for it.

item.description: Sweeping changes to Victorian rental laws will kick in next week, making it easier - and a little cheaper - for prospective tenants.

item.title: Government to use taxpayer funds to underwrite cost of petrol in bid to ease fuel crisis
item.description: T﻿he move is a bid to shore up fuel supply and bring down the cost of petrol.

item.description: "That will help control the costs in the supply chain."

item.title: Poll shows Australians blame Donald Trump for fuel crisis
item.description: P﻿rices at the pump are hurting voters and a huge majority of them are pointing the finger at US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Australian cosmetics giant Mecca handed $594,000 fine for failing to lodge audited financial reports on time
item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.title: RBA to end card surcharges as cash usage dwindles
item.description: T﻿he Reserve Bank said the fees were no longer influencing people to make more efficient payments.

item.title: 'Doesn't pass the pub test': Hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges
item.description: Inside the hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges.

item.title: 'It has been a privilege': Cusack's Furniture to close late 2026
item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.title: Phones, smartwatches and earbuds to be banned in every Victorian school after government extends device crackd
item.description: Victoria's nation-first phone ban was first introduced to government schools in 2020 and will now capture all schools, including private and Catholic, from January 28, 2027.

item.title: Three-month fuel excise cost passes Senate in bid to fight high prices
item.description: Fuel price relief has been made official but drivers are warned it will take time for the excise cut to be felt in their wallets.

item.title: As fuel excise set to be halved, when will fuel prices in Australia go down?
item.description: With key taxes being slashed, when will Australians finally see relief at the pump?

item.title: Western Australia enacts emergency fuel powers as PM urges calm
item.description: The state's six fuel suppliers will have 48 hours to hand over "sensitive information", or risk being fined up to $100,000 per day.

item.title: PM defends lacklustre national address
item.description: The speech is widely rumoured to involve financial assistance for businesses hit by soaring fuel costs.

item.title: What our readers thought of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's address to the nation
item.description: "A waste of time" – those were the words nine.com.au readers used over and over again.

item.title: Neo-Nazi forced offline after alleged threats to MPs
item.description: Joel Davis, 31, has been in custody on remand since his arrest in November for allegedly calling for the "rhetorical rape" of independent Wentworth MP Allegra Spender.

item.title: We were all set for prime time, then we were let down with a thud by our leaders
item.description: Opinion: In terms of doom and gloom, we're being bombarded at the moment with bad news.

item.title: New COVID strain circulating in Australia as it spreads across the globe
item.description: Experts say there are no signs the variant BA.3.2, otherwise known as "Cicada", poses a bigger health threat than other variations.

item.title: Shocking report blows the lid on where government funding is being spent
item.description: What's shocking is that 68 per cent of operators are unlicensed and one of those organisations received nearly $34 million in funding last financial year.

item.title: Spending cuts, tax reforms flagged in next month's federal budget
item.description: More and more Australians are draining﻿ their savings just to stay afloat as hopes for cost-of-living relief in next month's federal budget slowly fade.

item.title: Experts send dire fuel crisis warning as PM travels to Singapore to lock in supplies
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is travelling to Singapore in a bid to shore up Australia's fuel supply, trading on the country's reputation as a food bowl.

item.description: The rapper has drawn widespread condemnation for making antisemitic remarks and voicing admiration for Adolf Hitler.

item.description: D﻿irectly addressing a head of state's remarks is a dramatic break from protocol for the leader of the Catholic Church.

item.description: V﻿iktor Orban offered to be the "mouse" to the Russian president's "lion", the reported transcripts read.

item.description: The US appears ready to accept a plan that would leave the regime much better off than before.

item.description: Matt Canavan has called for new cities, more babies and a domestic space industry.

item.description: Officers first detected a motorcycle speeding along the Princes Freeway﻿ in Lara around 10pm yesterday.

item.title: 'Liquid gold': Queensland pushes to fast-track expansion of outback oil field in response to fuel crisis
item.description: Australia produces very little oil itself, getting most of it from Asian neighbours, but David Crisafulli wants that to change.

item.title: Long Island architect Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to murdering seven women and admits he killed another
item.description: A Long Island architect who led a secret life as a serial killer pleaded guilty to murdering seven women and admitted he killed an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

item.title: 'Ketamine Queen' gets 15 years in prison for selling Matthew Perry the drugs that killed him
item.description: Jasveen Sangha became the third defendant sentenced of the five people who have pleaded guilty in connection with the 54-year-old actor's 2023 overdose death.

item.title: US, Israel and Iran agree to a two-week ceasefire but much remains unclear and some attacks continue
item.description: US says it'll do "something like" last June's strikes on nuclear sites if Iran refuses to surrender enriched uranium voluntarily.

item.title: Today in History - April 9: Dictator drug lord surrenders after 10 days of listening to Van Halen
item.description: Panama dictator Manuel Noriega was found guilty on drug charges on April 9, 1992, three years after the US invaded his country to arrest him.

item.title: NATO chief says he understands Trump's 'disappointment' with allies over Iran
item.description: N﻿ATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte has attempted to smooth out the US president's anger in a White House meeting.

item.title: Sydney tech giant Canva snaps up two local AI companies as $60 billion company teases 'even bigger' announceme
item.description: A﻿ustralian tech giant Canva has snapped up two local artificial intelligence (AI) companies. But bigger news is on the way.

item.title: Pentagon told Pope's top diplomat to 'take its side' on US military ambitions: report
item.description: The Pentagon has said reports of a meeting between senior Department of Defence officials and the Vatican's representative in the US are "highly exaggerated and distorted".

item.title: George Clooney accuses Donald Trump of 'war crimes' after president's threat to wipe out Iranian civilisation
item.description: The Hollywood actor accused Trump of committing war crimes. His comments were heard loud and clear in Washington.

item.title: Newly created Polymarket accounts bet big on US-Iran ceasefire hours before Trump's announcement
item.description: A group of new accounts on the prediction market Polymarket made highly specific, well-timed bets on whether the U.S. and Iran would reach a ceasefire.

item.title: 'Take alternative routes': Iranian authorities warn ships of sea mines planted in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) shared a chart which urges commercial vessels to avoid sea mines which were deployed following the US and Israel's war against Iran.

item.title: Third country risks tearing apart US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again in response to Israeli attacks against the Hezbollah militant group ...

item.title: Overseas tourist fighting for life following cycling accident at popular Australian holiday spot
item.description: It's understood the woman had a helmet with her but it's unclear if she was wearing it at the time.﻿

item.title: 'A real horrible way for you to leave us': Police probe alleged murder of Perth man
item.description: A West Australian man is behind bars tonight accused of murdering a 69-year-old man he knew in Perth's north.

item.title: Higgins' relief as Lehrmann rape finding stands forever
item.description: As disgraced ex-Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann runs out of options to appeal a ruinous defamation loss, his alleged victim Brittany Higgins has called for change.

item.title: Old change, new fortune: Your old pre-decimal coins could be worth thousands
item.description: A﻿ coin auction in Sydney is expected to see bids hitting up to $50,000.

item.title: PM lands in Singapore on mission to cement fuel supply
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has touched down in Singapore, talking up the chances of a deal to lock in supply from the country that ships half of Australia's petrol.

item.title: UK and Norway led a military operation to deter Russian submarines in the North Atlantic
item.description: NATO countries have repeatedly expressed concern that Russia could use its fleet of spy ships to sabotage underwater cables on which global communications depend.

item.title: Was Sean 'Diddy' Combs making amateur porn? Lawyers argue yes in appealing conviction on prostitution-related
item.description: They claim "pornography production and viewing of this sort is protected by the First Amendment and thus cannot constitutionally be prosecuted".

item.description: One Nation is once again hot on the heels of the Liberals in the crowded upcoming Farrer byelection.

item.title: Midwife's colleague charged after baby boy dies following 2024 home birth
item.description: A woman was having a home birth when signs of complications and requests to be taken to the hospital were allegedly ignored.

item.title: Queensland man charged after police find $1.1 million stash of stolen diesel
item.description: The 48-year-old man allegedly used stolen fuel cards to pump fuel from unmanned diesel depots im Brisbane and Ipswich between December 2024 and December 2025.

item.title: Trump rages at some of his biggest former backers in lengthy Truth Social post
item.description: Donald Trump has lashed out at some of his most prominent former supporters in a long and angry post on Truth Social.

item.title: Europe's new registration system comes into effect today. Here's how that affects Aussie travellers
item.description: Here's how your next overseas trip could look a little bit different from today.

item.title: Schoolgirls left 'sick to the stomach' after primary teacher's persistent TikTok messages
item.description: The New Zealand Teachers Disciplinary Tribunal has censured Hamilton teacher Grant Daniel Spicer for serious misconduct and ordered that his register entry be annotated for two years.﻿

item.title: Man incited racial violence with Cronulla bashing calls
item.description: Ryder Roy Shaw, 20, used an alias to post two images on TikTok on December 15, 2025, that urged followers to "stand the f--- up" and riot at Cronulla Beach following the Bondi Beach terror attack.

item.title: Iran war to permanently scar global economy even if peace is reached: IMF
item.description: "Even in the best case, there will be no neat and clean return to the status quo ante."

item.title: Jealous partner not guilty of man's murder, court told
item.description: Gofal Baziad, 54, has pleaded not guilty to the 2004 murder of Jason Palmer, the ex-husband of his on-again-off-again partner Remmy.

item.title: The easy task millions are putting off that will slash hundreds from bills
item.description: Power bills have overtaken groceries as the biggest source of household stress, but many Australians could save by taking one simple step.

item.title: Donald Trump shares uncensored footage of Florida woman being bludgeoned to death with hammer in rogue Truth S
item.description: The US president described the brutal attack as "﻿one of the most vicious things you will ever see" as he shared CCTV of the incident on Truth Social.

item.title: Thieves steal one tonne of lobster in Western Sydney heist
item.description: "﻿This will not compromise operating hours and supply will remain unaffected as we continue investigating the incident," they said in a social media post.

item.title: US to automatically register young men into military draft
item.description: I﻿t is a cost-saving measure that would end decades of self-registration.

item.title: Grace Tame Foundation to close after five years due to problems 'sustaining long-term funding'
item.description: In a statement, the organisation revealed that securing funds had become "increasingly challenging."

item.title: Sammy the seal delights drivers after blocking traffic on Victoria's Mornington Peninsula
item.description: Police were called to direct traffic on Victoria's Mornington Peninsula after several reports of a live seal sunbathing on a busy roadway.

item.title: 'Difficult tradeoffs': ASX-listed Life360 culls jobs in AI restructuring
item.description: T﻿he CEO admitted "the roles people play are shifting".

item.title: Father of man killed in skydiving holds 'no animosity' against man guilty over accident
item.description: Experienced instructor Stephen Hoare, 37, and his tandem passenger, Alex Welling, 32, died while skydiving at Goulburn airport in southern NSW in 2021.

item.title: Police investigate 'suspicious death' of woman whose car was allegedly hit by a kangaroo
item.description: Police responded to reports that a woman had stopped breathing near Barkly Homestead after a car she was in was reportedly hit by a kangaroo.

item.title: Trump lashes 'dishonourable' move as strait remains shut
item.description: The US president said there were "reports" Iran was levying a toll for passing ships.

item.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Epstein and knowledge of his crimes
item.description: T﻿he US First Lady has rejected "completely false" stories claiming she knew of the sex trafficker's crimes.

item.title: Manhunt for convicted gang rapist who escaped Sydney hospital continues for third day
item.description: Authorities say that Michael Angok, who was convicted of the gang rape of a 14-year-old in Sydney's west, was awaiting deportation before escaping Bankstown Hospital yesterday. ﻿

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith displays to remain on show at Australian War Memorial following alleged war crime charges
item.description: While the famous display remains, a new updated panel acknowledging his arrest sits beside it.

item.title: Queensland vigilantes bash their own stolen ute with baseball bats as thief drives through fence of family hom
item.description: T﻿he thief got away, but not before reversing through the front fence of a family home.

item.title: 'There is no other option': Dire warning for Aussie businesses amid fuel crisis
item.description: Fresh data has laid bare the scale of the impact.

item.title: Trump threat to exit NATO still 'possible', warns former aide
item.description: Trump has continued lambasting the alliance over its stance in the Iran war despite meeting its top official.

item.title: Man, 47, charged with murder after 32-year-old allegedly shot dead in Melbourne high-rise complex
item.description: A 47-year-old man has been charged with murder after a 32-year-old man died in an alleged targeted shooting at a high-rise apartment complex in Melbourne's east﻿.

item.title: Vance warns Iran not to 'play' the US as he departs for negotiations aimed at ending their war
item.description: The US Vice President said Trump "gave us some pretty clear guidelines" on how talks should go, but he didn't elaborate, as he set off on Air Force Two for Pakistan.

item.title: Geraldton man fronts court after allegedly murdering Perth 'pop'
item.description: The 39-year-old accused from Geraldton, about four hours north of Perth, was arrested at the scene.

item.title: Why the end of the war won't solve the oil crisis
item.description: Since the US war with Iran began at the end of February, the price of oil has risen and fallen on the likelihood of it ending soon.

item.title: '136 per cent increase in the last week alone': Bike sales surge as commuters abandon their cars
item.description: And there's a high chance you already own one.

item.title: Today in History - April 11 | After ripping off 104 other women, his 105th wife tracked him down
item.description: A US man was jailed for an outrageous string of fraud and bigamy charges on April 11, 1983.

item.title: As the US and Israel's war with Iran simmers on, are we heading towards World War Three?
item.description: Some experts believe the global conflict could expand into a world war if certain conditions are met, while others believe we are already in the early stages of WW3.

item.title: Top Democrat demands Donald Trump submit to a dementia test
item.description: One of the most prominent Democrats in Congress has written to the White House doctor demanding Donald Trump take a cognitive test.

item.title: Four women describe sexual misconduct by Rep. Eric Swalwell, including a former staffer who says he raped her
item.description: A former staffer of a leading Democratic candidate for California governor, says that the politician raped her.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts return from moon with a splashdown to close out a record-breaking lunar voyage
item.description: The Artemis II crew have returned to Earth, splashing into the Pacific Ocean after a ten-day voyage to space.

item.title: 'Risk of serious burn injuries': Kettle recalled over glaring design fault
item.description: Australia's product safety watchdog issued a recall notice.

item.title: Kamala Harris 'thinking about' another run for president
item.description: Kamala Harris has said she is thinking about running for president again in 2028.

item.title: US Vice President arrives in Pakistan, for crucial talks
item.description: Hello and welcome to our rolling coverage of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East on Saturday, April 11....

item.title: Wife breaks down after kind-hearted husband killed in 'vicious' shooting in rural Victoria
item.description: Richard Willis, 65, had been fatally shot and buried in a "shallow grave" on his farm in Victoria's north-west.

item.title: Possible 'kill car' rammed after high-speed police pursuit through Sydney's west
item.description: Police foiled a potential deadly underworld plot after they allegedly found a handgun and a semi-automatic gun inside the car, which they believe is linked to multiple shootings and a firebombing.﻿

item.title: Offenders on the run after two men stabbed in Melbourne home invasion
item.description: The offenders are still on the run after attempting to steal luxury cars and jewellery.

item.title: Man arrested after allegedly mowing down, injuring group of motorbike riders in road rage incident
item.description: The driver allegedly struck a group of motorbike riders ﻿in Sydney's south-west, who then allegedly chased him down and assulted him.

item.title: Qantas flight diverted after strong smell reported
item.description: A Qantas plane bound for Perth landed unexpectedly at Adelaide Airport with paramedics on standby after passengers and crew noticed a strong smell.

item.title: Accused scammer gives thumbs up to the cameras before being charged in alleged $150,000 shopping centre scam
item.description: The 38-year-old was allegedly part of a group of people that targeted elderly women at shopping centres across NSW by pretending to help them.

item.title: Three dead after car flips on its side after slamming into a tree in Perth's east
item.description: The car was extracted from the bushes by emergency services, barely recognisable following the triple tragedy on Stoneville Road.

item.title: Talks between the US and Iran begin in Pakistan after earlier indirect discussions
item.description: Three-party talks with the US, Iran and Pakistan were started after a reduction in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon.
